description_long: This is a rustic wooden picket fence section, featuring seven vertical slats with rounded tops, evenly spaced and connected by two horizontal rails at the top and bottom. A single diagonal brace runs from the lower left to the upper right, providing structural support and a classic countryside aesthetic. The wood appears weathered, with a greenish-brown hue suggesting exposure to the elements or moss growth. The construction is simple, with no visible fasteners or decorative embellishments, emphasizing functionality over ornamentation. The fence is relatively short, likely intended as a boundary marker or garden enclosure in an urban or suburban setting. Its overall appearance is sturdy yet lightweight, suitable for temporary or movable installation.
description: A rustic wooden picket fence section with seven rounded slats, two horizontal rails, and a diagonal brace, finished in a weathered greenish-brown.
description_view_0: Front view showing all seven vertical slats, two horizontal rails, and the diagonal brace clearly.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with slats and rails aligned in a single plane.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same slat and rail arrangement, but with less visible detail due to lighting.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and layered construction.
